question: Explain why version control is considered a fundamental resource in a software engineering project, and list two specific tools used for this purpose.
expectedPoints: It enables collaboration by allowing multiple developers to work on the same codebase without conflicts.
It provides a complete history of changes, facilitating debugging and tracking progress.
Common tools include Git (with platforms like GitHub/GitLab) and Subversion (SVN).

question: Identify and briefly describe two types of learning resources a project manager could use to train a team on a new Agile methodology.
expectedPoints: Official Documentation/Guides: Resources like the Agile Manifesto or Scrum Guide provide the foundational principles and rules.
Online Courses/Tutorials: Platforms like Coursera or Udemy offer structured learning paths with video lectures and practical exercises.

question: Evaluate the role of a Requirements Management tool (like Jira or IBM DOORS) in the context of project management. Discuss how it impacts key areas such as scope management, traceability, and team communication.
expectedPoints: Scope Management: Provides a centralized repository for requirements, helping to prevent scope creep by formally tracking and approving changes.
Traceability: Enables linking requirements to design, implementation, and test cases, ensuring all stakeholder needs are met and simplifying impact analysis.
Team Communication: Serves as a single source of truth, improving transparency and alignment between developers, testers, and stakeholders through features like comments and dashboards.
